info.title: Oracle REST Data Services API
info.description: Oracle REST Data Services (ORDS) provides a REST interface for Oracle Database. It enables HTTPS access to database resources including tables, views, PL/SQL procedures, and custom REST modules. ORDS supports AutoREST for automatic RESTful access to database objects, custom resource modules with SQL/PLSQL handlers, OpenAPI catalog generation, OAuth2 authentication, and database management operations.

get.description: Returns the OpenAPI catalog of all available REST services
Get OpenAPI metadata for a specific resource
AutoREST: Retrieve rows from a REST-enabled table or view. Supports filtering, pagination, and field selection.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Filter query in JSON format, e.g. {"salary":{"$gt":50000}}

404.description: Object not found or not REST-enabled

post.description: AutoREST: Insert a new row into a REST-enabled table

get.description: AutoREST: Retrieve a specific row by its primary key or ROWID

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Row primary key value or ROWID

put.description: AutoREST: Update an existing row by primary key

delete.description: AutoREST: Delete a row by primary key

post.description: AutoREST: Batch load rows from CSV data into a REST-enabled table

get.description: Execute a custom resource module GET handler defined via ORDS.DEFINE_HANDLER

post.description: Execute a custom resource module POST handler

put.description: Execute a custom resource module PUT handler

delete.description: Execute a custom resource module DELETE handler

get.description: Get PDB open mode and status

post.description: Open, close, or change PDB status

get.description: List database objects (tables, views, procedures, etc.)
Get details of a specific database object
List all REST modules for a schema

post.description: Create a new REST resource module

get.description: List resource templates for a module

post.description: Obtain an OAuth2 access token using client credentials

source.description: SQL query, PL/SQL block, or JS function

basicAuth.description: HTTP Basic authentication with database credentials

bearerAuth.description: Bearer token from OAuth2 token endpoint
